name: Zyte Web Scraping & Data Extraction API
what_it_does: Enterprise web scraping platform with REST API offering AI-powered data extraction, browser rendering, and managed proxy rotation — built by the Scrapy team with structured data extraction (product, article, job listing) schemas and Scrapy Cloud for spider deployment.
use_cases: AI-powered structured data extraction from e-commerce product pages and job listings
Running Scrapy spiders at scale via Scrapy Cloud without managing infrastructure
Extracting product data with automatic field detection via Zyte API AI extraction
Bypassing bot detection with real browser rendering and residential proxies
Building large-scale web data pipelines with managed spider scheduling
not_for: Simple single-page scraping without scale (use ScraperAPI for simplicity)
Real-time data pipelines requiring sub-second latency
Sites with explicit ToS prohibition on scraping — verify legal compliance
best_when: An agent needs enterprise-grade structured web data extraction with AI-powered field detection across thousands of pages, especially for e-commerce data.
avoid_when: You need a simple one-URL-in, HTML-out scraping API — Zyte's AI extraction is more complex but more powerful.

auth.notes: API key via HTTP Basic auth (key as username, empty password). Simple authentication model. Single key per organization. Scrapy Cloud uses separate project-scoped tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free trial credits on signup; no ongoing free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-request: HTTP requests from $1.60/1000; browser from $6.40/1000; AI extraction higher
pricing.notes: Request-type-based pricing. AI extraction most expensive. Scrapy Cloud priced separately by spider compute units. Enterprise plans with discounts. Founded by Scrapy creators — strong open source heritage.

performance.rate_limits: Concurrent requests limited by plan; asynchronous API for batch requests

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAPI spec available. Comprehensive documentation with AI extraction schemas. Async API for batch requests with webhook callbacks. Error responses include detailed failure reasons and retry guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All scraping requests are stateless and idempotent. Same URL may return different content if target changes. Async job IDs for tracking batch requests.
known_agent_gotchas: AI extraction schemas (product, article, job) have specific field availability per site
Async mode required for batch scraping — synchronous mode limited to individual requests
Browser rendering significantly more expensive than HTTP mode — use selectively
Scrapy Cloud (spider hosting) uses different auth and billing than Zyte API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Basic auth (API key as username). No OAuth or fine-grained scopes. GDPR compliant. Legal compliance for scraping is user responsibility. No PII handling built-in.
